title: Orchestrator Prompt — Delivery (Act-3)
status_field: CANONICAL — Orvex Studio adaptation, ratified 2026-07-06 (PO batch approval)
what_it_is: The Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator operating prompt for Orvex Studio: a self-contained three-act lifecycle (Act 1 investigate + PLAN with a PO stop; Act 2 EXECUTE — write reconciled canon + structure Linear + build dashboards; Act 3 — AUTONOMOUSLY DELIVER the backlog to Done via a tick loop of claim → TDD build → adversarial review → deterministic Done gate → merge → advance). It carries the actual reusable delivery machinery cited by section number elsewhere: §0 pure-orchestrator/dispatch contract and model-tier table, §1 foundation doctrine (zero-mock, prod-parity, CI, k8s patterns, credential lanes), §2 Studio canon baseline (event envelope ADR-0007/0010, claim arbiter ADR-0006/0033, credential-lane ADR-0034, Linear ground truth), §3.1-3.33 operating doctrine (frontier computation, rolling-saturation concurrency, DoD-tick mechanics, quota discipline, capacity heartbeat), §6A the Act-3 tick loop itself, §7 Linear model, §8 command reference, and §13 dated lessons log. Adapted from Houston's delivery-orchestrator pattern.

target_pages: 5eFdxN3edd (Delivery Program — 4-phase model of record: Phase 0 Stabilize, Phase 1 Definition Factory, Phase 2 Isolated Builds, Phase 2.5/3)
overlaps.nature_of_overlap: Per the current program's own account, gkkUDzn277 is reusable machinery that the 4-phase Delivery Program's phase orchestrators (P1/P2/P2.5) invoke internally — it is NOT itself the top-level plan of record; 5eFdxN3edd is. But gkkUDzn277's own text gives zero indication of that containment relationship: it presents its own Act 1 -> Act 2 -> Act 3 as a complete, self-sufficient programme lifecycle, with its own 'STOP for PO Review' gate at the end of its own Act 1 (§5.6) and its own 'run to DONE' autonomy mandate (§3.16) — structurally indistinguishable, to a cold reader, from a competing top-level entry point. I grepped the full page body for any mention of 'Phase 0/1/2/2.5', 'Delivery Program', or the slug 5eFdxN3edd and found none. A reader who lands on gkkUDzn277 first (e.g. via the 'Delivery Prompts landing' KNZMCOKAV8 or a search hit) has no signal that this prompt now runs as a component invoked FROM WITHIN the 4-phase model rather than as the standalone programme orchestrator it reads as. That is exactly the confusion risk the PO is trying to eliminate: two documents each read as 'the' entry-point delivery orchestrator with no cross-reference stating which one is authoritative-at-the-top and which is machinery-invoked-from-within.

recommended_fix: Do NOT fold or supersede gkkUDzn277 — its content (credential lanes, claim arbiter, tick-loop mechanics, quota discipline, §13 lessons) is substantive, actively cited-by-section-number machinery that the newer phase prompts depend on, not a competing plan. Add a short banner near the top (after the existing CANONICAL/ratified line) stating: this page is the reusable Act-3 delivery-orchestrator machinery, invoked by the Delivery Program's Phase-1/Phase-2/Phase-2.5 orchestrator prompts (link 5eFdxN3edd + yXUWpQpRjx/Ng66su4dVG/ErYdXzIj6g); the Delivery Program page is the current plan-of-record entry point. This is a breadcrumb-only fix (ADD-CROSSREF), not a rewrite of the body doctrine. Separately (not on this page): the new parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock) should be folded into the Phase-2 'Isolated Builds' prompt (Ng66su4dVG) or added as a child page of 5eFdxN3edd — it does not belong merged into gkkUDzn277, which is engine-mechanics, not phase-sequencing/team-topology.
